amendments.text:
(a) Each cargo compartment must be designed for its placarded maximum weight of contents and for the critical load distributions at the appropriate maximum load factors corresponding to the flight and ground load conditions of this Part. (b) There must be means to prevent the contents of any cargo compartment from becoming a hazard by shifting, and to protect any controls, wiring, lines, equipment or accessories whose damage or failure would affect safe operations. (c) Where the cargo compartment is located aft of occupants and separated from them by structure, there must be means within the cargo compartment to protect the occupants from injury by the contents of the cargo compartment when the ultimate forward inertia force is 4.5g. (d) Cargo compartments must be constructed of materials which are at least flame resistant. (e) Designs which provide for cargo to be carried in the same compartment with the occupants must have means to protect the occupants from injury under the ultimate inertia forces specified in Sec. 23.561(b)(2). [(f) If cargo compartment lamps are installed, each lamp must be installed so as to prevent contact between lamp bulb and cargo.] Notice of Invitation to Submit Proposals; Notice No. 74-5; Issued on 2/14/74. (a) Each cargo compartment must be designed for its placarded maximum weight of contents and for the critical load distributions at the appropriate maximum load factors corresponding to the flight and ground load conditions of this part. (b) There must be means to prevent the contents of any cargo compartment from becoming a hazard by shifting, and to protect any controls, wiring, lines, equipment or accessories whose damage or failure would affect safe operations. [(c) There must be a means to protect occupants from injury by the contents of any baggage or cargo compartment, located aft of the occupants and separated by structure, when the ultimate forward inertia load factor is 9g and assuming the maximum allowed baggage or cargo weight for the compartment. ] (d) Cargo compartments must be constructed of materials which are at least flame resistant. [(e) Designs which provide for baggage or cargo to be carried in the same compartment as passengers must have a means to protect the occupants from injury when the cargo is subjected to the inertia loads resulting from the ultimate static load factors of Sec. 23.561(b)(3) of this part, assuming the maximum allowed baggage or cargo weight for the compartment.] (f) If cargo compartment lamps are installed, each lamp must be installed so as to prevent contact between lamp bulb and cargo. [(g) Baggage compartments used in computer category airplanes must also meet the requirements of paragraphs (a), (b), (d), and (f) of this section.] 86-19; Issued on 11/28/86. --> Link to eCFR --> NPRM ACTIONS:   Notice of Proposed Rulemaking. Notice No. 86-19; Issued on 11/28/86. 25147; Issued on 08/08/88. --> Link to eCFR --> FINAL RULE ACTIONS:   Final Rule. Docket No. 25147; Issued on 08/08/88.

[(a) Each baggage and cargo compartment must: (1) Be designed for its placarded maximum weight of contents and for the critical load distributions at the appropriate maximum load factors corresponding to the flight and ground load conditions of this part. (2) Have means to prevent the contents of any compartment from becoming a hazard by shifting, and to protect any controls, wiring, lines, equipment or accessories whose damage or failure would affect safe operations. (3) Have a means to protect occupants from injury by the contents of any compartment, located aft of the occupants and separated by structure, when the ultimate forward inertial load factor is 9g and assuming the maximum allowed baggage or cargo weight for the compartment. (b) Designs that provide for baggage or cargo to be carried in the same compartment as passengers must have a means to protect the occupants from injury when the baggage or cargo is subjected to the inertial loads resulting from the ultimate static load factors of Sec. 23.561(b)(3), assuming the maximum allowed baggage or cargo weight for the compartment. (c) For airplanes that are used only for the carriage of cargo, the flightcrew emergency exits must meet the requirements of Sec. 23.807 under any cargo loading conditions.] 94-21; Issued on 07/14/94. --> Link to eCFR --> NPRM ACTIONS:   Notice of Proposed Rulemaking. Notice No. 94-21; Issued on 07/14/94. 27806; Issued on 01/29/96. --> Link to eCFR --> FINAL RULE ACTIONS:   Final Rule. Docket No. 27806; Issued on 01/29/96.
